title: Cotswolds: Nether Westcote camp with family

main_route.excerpt: We stopped at Beckett's Farm for food.
We reached Nether Westcote at 12.45. It's a village, one of several, on the row of springs half way up the line of hills.
Monday — We chatted & had coffee & left at 11.30., bringing Aylan back to Birmingham

excursions.label: Sunday walk: Sledington (Bledington), River Evenlode, Idbury; supper at New Inn

stops.excerpt: We went through Sledington & Simon found a marvellous place for a picnic under a huge shady tree, by the R. Evenlode.
Simon found a marvellous place for a picnic under a huge shady tree, by the R. Evenlode.
We walked back out of the valley by road passing through Idbury.
In the evening we had supper at the New Inn, our campsite pub.

unresolved.issue: Person's name uncertain in transcription — appears as '[?Dylan]' on B_0009_R and 'Aylan' on B_0010_L; likely the same individual but identity unconfirmed.
unresolved.excerpt: Simon, John, Charles, Eve + Helen set off to go to the Cotswolds to camp with David, Jean + [?Dylan]

boundary_flags: B_0009_R begins mid-page with end of a previous trip (Ashford/train/M25 text); this trip starts at the 'Saturday August 23rd' heading.
B_0010_L ends mid-page; Monday return to Birmingham is the last entry for this trip.
summary: A short August bank-holiday weekend camping trip to Nether Westcote in the Oxfordshire Cotswolds, with a family gathering including friends David, Jean, Tim, Margaret and others. The group enjoyed a Sunday walk down into the Evenlode valley via Bledington, returning through Idbury, and ate at the New Inn campsite pub.
extraction_notes: The date Saturday 23 August 2003 is confirmed (23 Aug 2003 was a Saturday). 'Beckett's Farm' is almost certainly Becketts Farm at Wythall, a well-known farm shop on the A435 south of Birmingham on the route to the Cotswolds. 'Sledington' is very likely Bledington — a village on the River Evenlode close to Nether Westcote — the spelling may reflect the diarist's phonetic rendering or mishearing of the name. The person named '[?Dylan]' on B_0009_R appears as 'Aylan' on B_0010_L; treated as unresolved since the name is uncertain. Helen is listed as a traveller departing from Birmingham (named alongside Simon, John, Charles, Eve on B_0009_R) but is not otherwise identified in the family list. No ferry crossings — entirely domestic trip.
